feed.title: SAP Community - SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
feed.subtitle: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Subordinates transfer together with manager
entry.content:
Hi experts,

When position hierarchy is set as leading hierarchy, is there option to transfer subordinates together with their manager in case when manager is changing position. As current setup will automatically set higher level manager as default manager in Job info which is ok in some cases, but in some cases new record in job info with higher level position is redundant.

Even with position types added to manager position, subordinates are still getting record in job info higher level position.

Position type setup is attached. 

Thanks

Scenario:

  1. Manager is changing position via Job info insert new record or via All actions change job and compensation info.
  2. As manager moves to new position his subordinates are transferred to next level manager of now empty position which was occupied by last manager.
  3. Subordinates are now getting new record in job info with new supervisor that is employee from next level manager position.
  4. After positions of subordinates get changes in position objects relationship to connect with new manager position, they now have 2 records in Job info, one with next level position = redundant, and one that follows actual manager = ok.

entry.title: Onboarding - Rehire with old Employment Error
entry.content:
Hello,

We are trying to rehire a former employee, Luis Alberto Ramirez, into position 90000546. The action being used is Rehire with Old Employment.

However, when attempting to submit the hiring action, we encounter the following screen with an error:

Error:

An application error has occurred. Please try again later or contact support if the issue persists (include the following error information:
errorId=67273211-5abd-43a1-8a18-f962fa5bf052,
timestamp=2026-04-06T20:37:24.026+0000,
along with the version information of the current screen).

Has anyone experienced a similar issue or can provide guidance on what might be causing this?

entry.title: How to implement Data Privacy and Retention Architecture in Success Factors
entry.content:
Hi Community 

I would like to understand  how data retention rules are configured in your organisations. 

What configuration options are available within SuccessFactors to support retention and deletion
Any constraints or risks we should be aware of
Recommended approach and next steps to move this into delivery

Thanks

entry.title: Can we have person id set different than User ID via business rule or integration centre?
entry.content: Our client has a request that user id should not be same as person id . Is this achievable via business rule or integration centre ?

entry.title: Temporary Time Information not triggering a Data Replication Proxy
entry.content:
Due to the nature of our business, we have time information being maintained in customer WFM systems, that then gets integration to SF EC via API. No issues so far. Data lands in Temporary Time Information and gets replicated to IT2003 in ECP. The next time a load is done, and a change is made to existing Temporary Time Information, we have noticed that a data replication proxy was not being triggered, and hence no error in Data Replication Monitor and no error in ECP SLG1. Took a bit of digging and back and forth with SAP for me to work out, the issue was when the actual rostered time for a day was being updated, the update only happened to the 'Work Schedule: Temporary Change' and not too the Temp Time Information (see the screenshots):

So this is the work schedule assignment (manage data --> temporary time information). Note the creation and last modified date is 30/03/2026. That was when this record was originally created. Below is temp time loaded for 06/04/2026. First load was done on 30/03/2026

On 09/04/2026 a change was made to the temp time for 06/04/2026 and you can see in the 'work schedule: temporary change: the 'created on' is 30/03/2026 and the 'last modified' is now (correctly) showing 09/04/2026

The same can be seen when you look at the segment

What hadn't change for this day those, is the "container" in manage time --> 'temporary time information' as both the 'created' and 'last modified' date is still showing 30/03/2026

I worked out that the issue was with our load files not containing a record to update this. This has now been fixed, and we now don't have an issue.

We manage multiple different call centres, different clients etc. so rostered times change all the time. In my view not unusual. And now that we've found the issue, it is all working fine.

My concern is what SAP support said, and something I have not been able to wrap my head around. So seeing if the brains trust can clarify this for me:

[SAP Support] "....the development team identified a gap in the functionality inside Employee Central solution.  Changing the Individual Work Schedule assigned to the Temporary Time Information won't trigger the event for the creation of the Data Replication Proxy"

 - Yep, figured as much but we have found the solution. Now for the confusing bit:

[SAP Support] "We can confirm that after some days of having Temporary Time Data assigned to the employee, the Individual Work Schedule inside that object is being changed, and that won't be supported anymore."

How can this not be supported come May? In the words of Denzel Washington, "please explain this to me like I am a 6 year old" Rosters/schedules change all the time. That is the whole idea of "Temporary" in 'Temporary Time Assignment"....or am I missing something?

entry.title: Latest People Profile impact on Data Imports
entry.content:
Hello Everyone-

One Question Regarding Latest People Profile & 1H/2026 updates.

Will the  Latest People Profile & 1H/2026 updates have any impact on the way we import data? I mean "Import and export data" & "Import Employee Data "? Please clarify on this.

Regards 

Karthik

entry.title: How can I modify custom MDF cards on the Latest People Profile
entry.content:
Hi,

One of my customer will change from the legacy people profile to the Latest People Profile with the new release.

Regarding to this, they started to test it, and for this, I created some custom MDF cards, but it seems that somehow it is not too informative:

Is there any way to modify it? The Configure Latest People Profile is really limited in this aspect and my customer wants to see more things on the cards. Is there a workaround? Or it is permission related issue?

Thank you for your hel in advance

SAP SuccessFactors Platform SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central

Norbert

entry.title: Automatic Wage calculation as per New Labour Code
entry.content: Hi Puja, Did you achieve the following requirement in EC?

entry.title: Onboarding 2.0 - External to Internal Hires Behavior
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I just want to confirm that during ConvertExternalUserOnStartDateJob, if for cases that start date is still in future date, meaning candidate is still tagged as inactive in the system, it is still possible to edit his/her employee details, right?

As for the access, I also want to validate that candidates turn into employee can only access the system or can log in the system once they are active or at the start of their hire date?

Thank you very much!

 

Best Regards,

Gelo

entry.title: Compensation Promotion with Position Management enabled
entry.content:
Hello Compensation experts (and @xavierlegarrec ), 

I have been wondering what the leading practice is/should be in terms of enabling the promotion tool in the Compensation module, especially when Position management is in place. 

From my understanding (I am not an EC/Position Management expert), it is usually accepted as a best practice that fields that are managed at the position level (like job classification, pay grade etc) should not be changed directly in the Job information portlet. 

However, when promoting an employee in compensation, selecting a new job classification or pay grade is often exactly what the compensation planner wants to do. So this is where i get confused: if we should not update fields in job info, then we cannot really publish the promotions recorded in compensation to the job info portlet. The only way we could publish them would be to have positions created in advance of the compensation planners recording the promotions, and the planners would then have to chose the right position for the employee to be promoted to. This seems very impractical. 

So far what i have done is to use the promotion tool in compensation to record the promotions suggested  by the planner, but these do not get published automatically and instead, and admin has to process the promotions manually/via imports by first creating the positions and then updating the job information to the correct new position. Is there a better way to do this according to you? 

Also, on a similar topic, in some instances, it is desirable for customers to have the possibility to change the pay grade or job classification of an employee without changing their positions. For instance a member of their team started 2 years ago as a Junior accountant, and should now be promoted to associate accountant, but without changing position/responsibilities (similar to the pay scale functionality). Is this possible with Position Management enabled?

Thanks a lot for your insights

entry.title: API for Employee central document Generation
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

Good day!

We are checking on different options to automate confirmation letter generation ( Mainly using AI agents) in SuccessFactors. For the same, we need External API for Employee central document generation in SuccessFactors. 

I have got one KBA (https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3261843) stating there is no API for document generation.  

Any suggestions? 

Thank You,

Vinoli

entry.title: CEO Data Missing After Update Worksheet in SAP SuccessFactors Annual Bonus Template
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing an issue in SAP SuccessFactors Compensation while working with the Annual Bonus process.

Scenario

  • CEO data is successfully imported through Employee History.
  • The record is available before running the Update Worksheet process.
  • After executing Update Worksheet, the CEO data is missing from the final Annual Bonus calculation report/template.

Observation

We noticed that the CEO does not have a second manager assigned in the hierarchy.

Because of this, we are unsure whether:

  • the worksheet update process is skipping the CEO record,
  • or if there is any hierarchy limitation/standard behavior in SuccessFactors Compensation.

Questions

  1. How should bonus calculation be handled for CEO records when there is no second manager in the hierarchy?
  2. What is the recommended approach to manage CEO records during the Update Worksheet process?
  3. Is there any standard SAP SuccessFactors configuration or workaround for this scenario?
  4. Has anyone faced a similar issue with executive/CEO data disappearing after Update Worksheet?

Any suggestions or guidance would be very helpful.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: How to effectively configure the "Employment Differentiator" to distinguish multiple employments?
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a question regarding the best way to distinguish multiple employment records. 

In our SuccessFactors Employee Central and Performance & Goals environment, we have users with Concurrent Employments, and it's becoming difficult for managers to tell which record they are selecting in the Employment Switcher. 

  1. Currently, we are using Company, Department, and a custom field for Expatriate/Concurrent status.
    However, we’ve encountered a challenge: some employment records are created solely for workflow/approval purposes. This leads to cases where a single employee has multiple records with identical Company, Department, and Expatriate/Concurrent status (as shown in the attached image).

  2. We have considered creating a custom field to manually flag the purpose of each employment record (e.g., "For Approval Only"). However, this would significantly increase the administrative workload for our HR team. Furthermore, we are concerned that manual entries might not be intuitive enough for users to understand at a glance.

    Any advice, screenshots, or links to implementation guides would be greatly appreciated!
    Thanks in advance for your help.

entry.title: How to enable proxy management feature
entry.content:
Login in instance > Navigate to proxy management >Scroll down > Enable Advanced proxy management (Proxy now and proxy import) > Will get Proxy now feature in home page
Another way
Go to Settings > Click on proxy > Click on Become proxy >Proxy any username

entry.title: After the new release, employees cannot reach their employee profile
entry.content:
Hi,

On saturday the new release had been launched by the SAP. On sunday, I did some changes and the system worked properly. 

However, today morning, around 9 am CET, my customer let me know that they cannot reach the employee profiles. 

Around 8 am CET, somebody said that he was able to reach it so it is really confusing. 

Now, including me, who has superadmin access, so I should see everything, I see just the same as the other employees.

 

Do you have any information about SuccessFactors Ouateges?

 

Thank you,

Norbert

entry.title: "Internal error; contact system administrator" when trying to send email to imported employees IAS
entry.content: This error message shows when we try to send emails to the imported users on the cloud identity services platform. Any help please?

entry.title: My customer's employee cannot log in into SuccessFactors AGAIN!
entry.content:
Hi,

After the yesterday morning ouatege, my customer cannot log in into the SuccessFactors system again, due to invalid login. I also cannot log in into the instance nor the test system or to the IAS. Do you have any updates or news with this from other countires?

Thank you!

Norbert

entry.title: Failing the SAP SuccessFactors System-Based Certification Exam Despite Correct Task Execution
entry.content:
Hello,

I’ve taken the Configuring SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Core and Position Management system-based certification exam twice now, and unfortunately I’m still failing it. What’s confusing to me is that during the exam, most of my configurations and tasks seem to work exactly as requested in the objectives. The execution and results appear correct, so I’m struggling to understand where I’m losing points.

One thing I’m especially unsure about is the handling of data models and DTD files during the exam. In the exam environment, the DTD files are not provided to me when working with data models. Should I ignore this fact during the exam, or is there a way we’re expected to obtain those files within the system?

For those who have passed system-based SAP exams before:

  • Are there common mistakes that are overlooked even when the task appears to work?

  • Does SAP grade based on specific configuration methods rather than just the final result?

  • How strict is the validation/grading process?

  • Are there hidden requirements or validation points that are easy to miss?

I’d really appreciate any advice or insight from people who’ve gone through this experience. I’m trying to understand what I might be doing wrong before attempting the exam again.

Thank you.

entry.title: SAP Improvement - Improve Purge Preview Report and Complete Report input data of DRTM Workflow Purge
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have a new improvement raised during March 2026 in SAP Customer Influence. See the link below:

Request ID: 369284 - Improve Purge Preview Report and Complete Report input data of DRTM Workflow Purge Type

We raised it due to currently in SAP SF the "DRTM Workflow Purge type" when checked the preview and complete report input data is not sufficient information to validate which workflows related to the employee are being removed. Only the summed amount.

Our request is to improvement of the input data with further information.

 


For example an entry per each workflow the person will get/have purged with:
Workflow ID;
Workflow Name.

This way it will be easier to understand the report and information that it will be removed from the user.

Thanks in advance for your support.

Cláudia Velez

entry.title: SAP Successfactors Employee Central - RBP Optimization
entry.content:
Role-Based Permissions (RBP) is a security model that allows you to restrict and grant access to your SAP SuccessFactors HCM suite. RBP controls access to the applications that employees can see and edit.

There’re four concepts regarding RBP:

  • Permission role: Define what actions a user can perform and what data they can access e.g., approving timesheets, or editing compensation
  • Granted population: Define who gets the permission e.g., all HR Managers
  • Target population: Determine whose data the Permission Group can act upon e.g., a manager's target population is their direct reports
  • Role assignment: A relationship between a permission role and its access population and target population

Steps for RBP Optimization:

  • Review Overlapping Permissions in Roles.
  • Review and remove group with zero members
  • Remove Inactive Roles
  • RBP Reports

Recommendations for RBP Optimization:

  • More use of Dynamic Groups than Static Groups
  • Reduce the number of Permission Groups
  • Reduce the number of RBP Administrator
  • Naming Conventions for Roles and Groups
  • Use RBP Check Tool
  • RBP Troubleshooting Tool

Overlapping Permissions in Roles

Different employees have the same permissions granted several times in different roles assigned that means when an employee wants to use a functionality, the system validates the permissions granted to that functionality in each role granted to the employee and for the different target populations defined, which incurring performance issues.

Recommendations

  • The focus when creating roles and groups should be the permission and not the employee means roles should be defined based on the set of functionalities that are required to perform an activity and then grant that role to the necessary users.
  • Review the Check Tool and investigate roles that duplicate permissions for the specified user in the exported results.

Use of Dynamic Groups than Static Groups

Use of Dynamic groups will add the employee to the required granted group automatically instead of manually adding the employee, also increases system performance.

Choosing the required people pool criteria or custom flags helps in creating the dynamic groups.

Remove Redundant Groups and Roles

Remove the groups with zero active employees and inactive roles to streamline maintenance and help to avoid system performance issues.

Reduce the number of RBP Administrator

Having many people involved in updating and maintaining RBP can cause security issues or user errors if not communicated and documented appropriately.

Maintain RBP workbook to track and maintain the RBP provides a quick reference for group and role design, helps to avoid overlapping configuration, and is a good resource when planning updates or overhauls of how your configuration is done.

RBP Naming Convention

Meaningful group and role names and role descriptions help customers to identify the correct groups and roles later during maintenance and troubleshooting.

Not naming your groups indicating whether they are Granted or Target can lead to a lack of understanding on what the Groups do and to duplicity of the same.

Example: GG_HRAdmin & TG_HRAdmin

RBP Reports

Reports help to troubleshoot and understand the permissions that have been configured. RBP Table reports are an aggregate of all the RBP data in your system.

KBA - https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-platform/implementing-role-based-permissions/rbp-table-reports

Use of Check Tool

The Check Tool can check your configuration and identify any potential problem areas, and recommend steps to resolve those issues, with or without submitting a support ticket. It’s beneficial to run RBP checks after:

  • You make a major set of changes to RBP.
  • You complete an instance refresh.
  • You use Instance Sync.

KBA - Using the Check Tool to Solve Issues | SAP Help Portal

RBP Troubleshooting Tool

We can use RBP Troubleshooting to better prevent, diagnose, and fix RBP issues.

Details of the RBP Troubleshooting:

  • User Role and Permission Search allow you to search for and compare permission roles and user permissions. You're able to search for the roles and permissions of a single user. Alternatively, you can search for and compare the roles and permissions of two users.
  • Target Population Search allows you to search for the target populations that an employee user can access or manage based on specific permissions.
  • Compare Roles allows you to search for permissions of a role or compare the permissions of two roles.
  • User Group Search allows you to search the permission groups that the user belongs to. It includes the following information: group ID, group name, user type, and group type.

Benefits:

  • Increases system performance
  • Avoid Redundancy
  • Better governance
  • Reduces maintenance of Permission roles and groups 
  • Security
  • Limited Roles and Groups
